schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: A thin web presentation surface (Law 7) over the running system — the devices on the heartbeat across the TOP (the roster, child c), the selected device's ACTIVE page BELOW (its panes: STATUS / SETTINGS floor + declared interaction/logging, child a). Three parts under one charter: (1) render.py — PURE functions `data -> html` (nav, panes, document), no I/O, no socket, everything a device says HTML-escaped; (2) server.py — `WebServerDevice`, a Form v0 #2 device whose `serve(path) -> (status, content_type, html)` routes a request to a rendered page, reaching each device's `active_page()` live THROUGH the ground_loop that beats it (in-process v0); (3) daemon.py — the thin http.server wrapper binding a LOCALHOST port and handing each GET to `serve`, the OS-specific piece the proof does not exercise. The web server owns NO device state: `/` is the landing (nav + pick-a-device), `/device/<id>` is that device's ACTIVE page, an unknown device is a loud coherent 404 that still renders the nav.
why: Telos 2 — Cairn has a USER; the librarian needs a web interface, and 'a web interface falls out of the chatbot' only if the surface exists first. This is that surface, on the spine BEFORE the librarian (MAP.md build order: web server → harbor_master → librarian-as-chatbot). It is the Form v0 #2 introspection surface made concrete — the SAME protocol the tester probes and the web UI renders, so observability is not a separate build. Built as a PRESENTATION surface (Law 7: it may collapse an error into a coherent shape — the 404, the absent-pane reason — but holds no record of truth and produces no DATA of its own), which is exactly what keeps the intelligence in the shims and the web server trivial (Law 6: one owner, no state). Because it renders DATA the devices produce (never markup a device authored), a hostile string in a device's feed can never become the page's markup — escaping is physics here, not diligence.
invoke: import: `from cairn.web_server.server import WebServerDevice` (over a ground_loop) and `from cairn.web_server import render`. Prove: `python3 cairn/web_server/proofs/test_web_server.py` (exit 0 = green), or under the tester. Run the daemon: `python3 cairn/web_server/daemon.py [--port 8787]` — binds localhost, serves an honest empty nav until the launcher wires the real running heartbeat.
node_class: code-seam (a spine device by role — the web presentation surface, after inference_domain in the build order). web-server ticket child b; children a + c landed in base + ground_loop respectively.
entry: FRESH design, standard mechanism, converged with Akien 2026-07-21 (the web_server design conversation: surfaces-in-the-shim, the data/render split, the artifact-owner spinoff, 'i think web server is /sorted'). The mechanism is ordinary Python stdlib (http.server, html.escape, json) — nothing crossed as carried code; UU's web surfaces are not grafted. The intelligence is deliberately elsewhere (the panes are child a's BaseShim machinery; the nav is child c's ground_loop roster), so this device is a bridge + frame renderer and little else — minimalism by construction.
state: PROVED for the route + render core, green under the tester (2026-07-22, test_web_server.py, 6 teeth): the nav IS the roster (every device on the heartbeat, in order, awake/asleep); a device page renders its panes pulled live through the heartbeat; an unknown device is a loud coherent 404 that still renders the nav (Law 7); every device-derived value is ESCAPED (a `<script>` in a device's state renders as text, never live markup); an absent pane renders its reason (child a's absent-with-reason survives to the surface); the web server owns NO state (it is a Form v0 #2 device, its state pulled live). The daemon binds a real localhost socket and serves (smoke-tested: 200 with the empty nav, 404 for an unknown device) — kept OUT of the proof on purpose (the socket is the thin OS wrapper). OPEN EDGES, filed not faked: (a) THE BUS TRANSPORT — v0 reaches devices in-process through the ground_loop (the whole spine's shared separate-process deferral); fetching active_page over the bus (a request/reply envelope to the device's personal channel, the shim answering) lands when devices are separate OS processes. (b) REPORTED-vs-FILED DRIFT on the STATUS pane — child a left the reported intention as DATA; comparing it against the filed intention+why.json on disk is this surface's render-time job (it reads disk intentions) and is not yet wired. (c) INTERACTION panes bringing their own rich view — v0 renders every pane's DATA as pretty JSON; the librarian's tool/chat page is the first real puller. (d) the JOURNEY pane (waits on harbor_master) and (e) the SETTINGS circuit-breaker (waits on the cgroup-kill sudo script) graft in when those exist. (f) the LAUNCHER wiring the real running heartbeat (instance-space, ~/.cairn/devices/web_server/0/) — daemon.py stands up an empty stub today.
gates: proof gate (tester) — proofs/test_web_server.py, run by the tester; verdict+seal from the same hand. Durability gate (code-seam): not done until proven, VALIDATION persisted beside-code (validations/test_web_server.json), AND committed. The socket daemon is exempt by construction (the unprovable-without-the-OS thin wrapper) — the route+render logic under it carries the proof.
falsifier: the surface is honest and thin — RED on any of: (1) a device's ACTIVE page shows a pane it did NOT declare, or omits the STATUS/SETTINGS floor (the pane set is child a's, not the web server's to invent); (2) a device-derived string reaches the page as live markup (escaping failed — Law 7, and a real injection); (3) the web server accretes its own state/cache of device internals (it becomes a second record of truth — the state() must stay pulled-live); (4) an error renders as a raw crash/stack instead of a coherent shape (Law 7's presentation half); (5) a device NOT on the heartbeat is viewable, or one ON it is not (the nav must be exactly the roster). Wrong-shape signal: if rendering logic migrates INTO the shims (shims emitting HTML instead of DATA), the data/render split has failed and the tester can no longer probe the same protocol the UI renders.
horizon: first REAL render — when the daemon serves the actual running heartbeat (the launcher wires it) with at least two live devices' ACTIVE pages pulled over the bus, STATUS reported-vs-filed drift-detection works, and the LOGGING pane reads real emission. Re-check then: is BaseShim the right home for the pane machinery, or do too many devices override it? And revisit the data/render split the first time an INTERACTION pane needs a rich device-provided view (the librarian's tool page).
how_it_learns: As a presentation surface, its learning is the DRIFT it makes visible, not a model it trains. STATUS puts a device's reported intention beside its filed one — the reported-vs-filed comparison (open edge b) is a live drift detector (Law 3: a device that has quietly become something other than its charter says shows up on its own page). The surface itself does not learn autonomously — by design, it holds no state to learn from; the learning it SERVES is every device's, made observable. Its own horizon: today it renders introspection DATA; when interaction panes bring rich views and the LOGGING pane reads real emission, the surface deepens as its consumers (the librarian's chat/tool page) pull them.
traces_to: Telos 2 (Cairn has a USER — the librarian's web interface rests on this surface)
Form v0 #2 (MAP.md:157 — the uniform introspection surface; one protocol for tester + web UI)
Law 7 (a presentation surface may collapse an error into a coherent shape; a record of truth never may — and never lets a device's data become markup)
Law 6 (the web server owns no state — content is each device's, reached through the heartbeat that holds it)
Law 4 (escaping + the data/render split are physics, not diligence)
Law 1 (every later surface is a read-projection of the panes + roster, not re-derived)
build order: web server, after inference domain (MAP.md build order)
owner: CC owns web_server's code + proofs and gates writes to them. Akien owns the design ratification (surfaces-in-the-shim, the data/render split, 'i think web server is /sorted', 2026-07-21). The listening PORT is owned by the web_server device (Law 6); the socket is the daemon's.
proof: proofs/test_web_server.py — the nav is the roster; a device page renders its panes pulled live through the heartbeat; an unknown device is a coherent 404 that still renders the nav; every device string is escaped; an absent pane renders its reason; the web server owns no state (a Form v0 #2 device, state pulled live). Composes a REAL ground_loop + REAL BaseShims + a REAL device, so it proves route → fetch-through-the-heartbeat → render WITHOUT a socket. Green: `python3 cairn/web_server/proofs/test_web_server.py`.
provenance: Born 2026-07-22 as the next step of the code spine after inference_domain, building the web-server ticket (cast 2026-07-21 via /sorted). Children a (BaseShim pane machinery) and c (ground_loop roster) landed first as the two DATA providers, each proven + persisted beside-code; b (this device — the daemon that renders them) is the third buildable child. Children d (journey pane) and e (circuit breaker) remain deferred on their grafts (harbor_master; the cgroup-kill sudo script). Advances Telos 2 from thesis toward a real surface; does not yet serve the live running heartbeat (the launcher wiring is the filed instance-space edge).
